issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status, which means it will include all transactions regardless of their status.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can make the query less readable and harder to maintain.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which can make it harder for others to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) AS total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) AS txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
  AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
  AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue by not filtering completed transactions and uses an implicit join syntax. It also lacks comments for readability.
